hero.title:
The Global Learning
Qualifications Framework
hero.subtitle: A flexible, open-source standard for defining and assessing college-level learning — wherever, however, and whenever it happens.

overview.heading: What is the GLQF?
overview.p1: The GLQF is built on research spanning more than 90 national qualification frameworks from around the world. It establishes a shared, global language for what it means to learn at the undergraduate level.
overview.p2: Whether learning happens in a classroom, through workplace experience, via open educational resources, or through independent study, the GLQF provides a common standard to recognize and evaluate it.
overview.quote: A common standard for college-level learning — independent of where, how, or when it was acquired.

constructsSection.heading: Three overarching constructs
constructsSection.intro: Together, these constructs describe what distinguishes college-level learning from knowledge or skill alone.

constructDescriptions.integration: Reflecting on, synthesizing, and combining knowledge to make sense of, unify, and share information within collaborative and evolving environments.
constructDescriptions.knowledge: The culmination of facts, concepts, principles, skills, and competencies acquired through experience and education that yield theoretical and practical understanding.
constructDescriptions.engagement: Purposefully interacting with, attending to, and developing a relationship with an object of study, a community, a project, or an assortment of tasks.

diagramSection.heading: Constructs and domains, integrated
diagramSection.intro: Three constructs at the center, eight learning domains around them. Select any label to dive deeper.

domainsSection.heading: Learning, broken down
domainsSection.intro: Each domain is defined at two levels — introductory (associate) and advanced (bachelor's) — giving evaluators, faculty, and learners clear, actionable descriptors.

internationalSection.heading: Grounded in 90+ national frameworks
internationalSection.intro: The policies, procedures, and qualification frameworks of more than 90 countries were researched in the development of the GLQF.

cta.heading: Use it. Adapt it. Build on it.
cta.body: The GLQF is freely available and fully adaptable. Use it to assess prior-learning portfolios, evaluate workplace training, design curriculum, award credit for open courseware, or benchmark microcredentials. Institutions, industry partners, and learners can apply it in whole or in part, and are encouraged to build upon it for their own contexts.

footer.tagline: The Global Learning Qualifications Framework — an open standard for defining and assessing college-level learning.

diagram.title: Global Learning Qualifications Framework
diagram.desc: Interactive diagram of the GLQF. Three core constructs — Integration, Knowledge, and Engagement — sit at the center of a sphere, surrounded by eight learning domains: Specialized Knowledge, Applied Knowledge, Integrated Knowledge, Communication, Information Literacy, Ethical Responsibility, Sociocultural and Civic Engagement, and Learning Engagement. Each label links to its detail page.
